name: Anemone
description: While sometimes considered a threat to coral reefs in high densities, it is a native species and a significant part of the ecosystem. Its presence is notable in the extensive reefs of MIMAROPA.

name: Whale Shark
description: The largest fish in the world, these gentle giants filter-feed on plankton. They are known to aggregate in certain areas of the Philippines, including waters accessible from MIMAROPA.
location: Open waters off Palawan and potentially other parts of MIMAROPA during migration periods..

name: Rabbitfish
description: A popular breakfast fish, often dried, abundant in the shallow waters around Marinduque and Mindoro, associated with seagrass beds and reefs. Multiple species within the genus Siganus are referred to as Danggit.
location: Shallow waters around Marinduque and Mindoro.

name: Dugong
description: A gentle, herbivorous marine mammal that grazes on seagrass beds. Palawan is a significant habitat for this vulnerable species in the Philippines.
location: Seagrass beds around Palawan, as well as parts of Mindoro and Romblon.

name: Roundscad
description: A staple fish in Filipino diets, caught in various parts of MIMAROPA.

name: Green Sea Turtle
description: A large sea turtle species that feeds on seagrass and algae. MIMAROPA hosts important nesting sites and foraging grounds for green sea turtles.
location: Nesting beaches and coastal waters of Palawan, Mindoro (e.g., Sablayan), and other islands in the region.

name: Grouper
description: High-value reef fish prized for their firm, white flesh, abundant in the extensive coral reefs of Palawan and other islands in MIMAROPA.
location: Coral reefs throughout Palawan, Mindoro, Marinduque, and Romblon.

name: Irrawaddy Dolphin
description: A small, toothed whale found in coastal areas and estuaries. While not strictly marine, they inhabit brackish waters connected to the sea. A population is known to exist in Malampaya Sound, Palawan.

name: Snapper
description: Premium fish with firm white flesh, commonly found in the waters around Palawan and Romblon, associated with reefs and deeper fishing grounds.
location: Coastal waters around Palawan and Romblon.

name: Stingray
description: Graceful filter-feeding rays often seen gliding through the water. They are known to frequent reef areas in Palawan.
location: Reefs and open waters around Palawan.

name: Hawksbill Sea Turtle
description: A critically endangered sea turtle with a distinctive hooked beak, often found in coral reef areas feeding on sponges and other invertebrates.
location: Coral reefs throughout Palawan, Romblon, and other parts of MIMAROPA.

name: Giant Clams)
description: The largest living bivalve mollusk, found in coral reefs. Palawan is known for its populations of giant clams, some of which are part of conservation efforts.
location: Coral reefs of Palawan and other islands in MIMAROPA.

name: Mackerel
description: Commercially important pelagic fish found in the waters of MIMAROPA.
location: Coastal and offshore waters of MIMAROPA.

name: Spanish Mackerel
description: A larger mackerel species favored for its firm texture and rich flavor.
